Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-20 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Data Today's data reveals two exceptionally strong performers with contrasting risk profiles. TYRA ranks first with a composite score of 97.7, displaying remarkably consistent momentum across all timeframes (244.78% 1-year return) with perfect SMA alignment and all five ratio consistency checks passing; notably, it maintained strong outperformance versus SPY (+230.49% annually) despite a concerning bad_spy_score of 0.52, suggesting it underperforms during market downturns. TNGX, the second entry, shows extreme volatility with a staggering 1003.47% one-year return but reveals significant momentum degradation—the ratio_1w_1d of -8.69 signals a recent pullback despite all SMA gates remaining green, and critically, its bad_spy_score of only 0.04 indicates it collapses severely when SPY declines, making it highly directional and fragile. While TYRA appears to offer more stable short-term momentum with better resilience metrics, TNGX's exceptional absolute returns come packaged with dangerous concentration risk and market-dependent performance. Both securities significantly outpaced QQQ on a 1-year basis, though the quality and sustainability of these gains differ substantially between the two leaders.
